This needs to be said separately, because it is not a disclaimer. It is **honesty.**

***

## What It States

The way Carror OS was built is the same as what it governs:

    Human: sets direction, makes rulings, establishes iron laws
    AI: implements, writes, executes, generates documentation

This means:

*   Hook logic is written by AI — there may be edge cases not covered
*   Documentation is generated by AI — there may be imprecise expressions
*   SKILL.md specifications are drafted by AI — there may be internal inconsistencies
*   Version numbers, field names, interface contracts may drift through iterations

This is not a quality issue. It is **honest disclosure of the construction method.**

***

## Why It Should Be at the Very Beginning of Marketing Documents

Because if you do not say it, wrong expectations will be created.

Users see 32 hooks, a精密 state machine, complete interface contracts, and assume this is a product polished by an engineering team. Then they discover a hook bug in a specific scenario, a field name in a document that does not match the code, a feature description that does not match the actual behavior — and they lose trust.

But if you are upfront from the start:

> **The decisions in this system are made by a human. The implementation is done by AI. One person's energy and AI's reliability are both limited. You will encounter inconsistencies, edge cases, documentation deviations. This is known, not neglect.**

Then users calibrate their expectations correctly. They will use it with the mindset of "this is a genuinely usable tool, but I need to verify things myself," rather than "this should be as perfect as commercial software."

***

## This Statement Itself Embodies the Carror OS Spirit

Iron Law #7: Assertions must be true.

A tool built with AI honestly discloses in its marketing document that "AI participated in building it, reliability is limited" — this is applying its own governance principles to itself.

Not concealing the construction method to make the product look better.

***

## The Opening Paragraph of Marketing Documents Should Read Something Like This

    Carror OS is a system built by one person.

    All core decisions — iron laws, mechanism design, architecture choices —
    are proposed or ruled on by a human.
    All implementation — hook writing, documentation generation,
    specification drafting — is done by AI.

    This means you may encounter:
      - Hooks with uncovered edge cases
      - Slight deviations between document descriptions and code behavior
      - Interface inconsistencies across version iterations

    This is not neglect. It is the honest result of the construction method.

    Individual energy is limited. AI reliability is limited.
    Please use it with this understanding. Open an issue when you find a problem,
    rather than expecting it to be as perfect as commercial software.

    It is genuinely usable. It is also genuinely imperfect.

***

## Finally

Putting this paragraph at the front will not make users lose confidence.

On the contrary, it will make those who truly understand the tool trust it more —

Because a tool willing to articulate its own limitations

will not lie to you elsewhere.
